title: lantana triplinervia var. longibracteolata
intro: Lantana triplinervia var. longibracteolata, also known as the long-bracted shrub lantana, is a plant species within the verbena family that is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Lantana triplinervia var. longibracteolata include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve making teas, infusions, or tinctures from its leaves, which can be used to treat various ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential side effects.
